title: Flow of Command-Line Argument Processing in a C Program
description: This flowchart illustrates how command-line arguments are passed from the shell to the main() function in a C program and how they are typically accessed and used.
relationships: The shell executes a program with arguments, which are passed to the program's main() function. The integer argc contains the total count of arguments. The array of pointers argv holds each argument as a string, starting with the program's own name at argv[0]. The program logic then uses these values for its execution.
